meta-description: Learn about supplemental security income by reviewing the definition in the HealthCare.gov Glossary.
title: Supplemental Security Income (SSI)
excerpt: A monthly benefit paid by Social Security to people with limited income and resources who are disabled, blind, or 65 or older. SSI benefits aren't the same as Social Security retirement or disability benefits.
content:
A monthly benefit paid by Social Security to people with limited income and resources who are disabled, blind, or 65 or older. SSI benefits aren't the same as Social Security retirement or disability benefits.

Related content

  • Learn more about SSI benefits, including how to apply, from the Social Security Administration.
